rationale_for_baseline_tier: The dermatological condition is benign, chronic vitiligo — classic sharply-demarcated symmetrical depigmentation with leukotrichia, normal sensation and no scaling or active border — which is telehealth-recognisable and managed with reassurance, sun protection and ROUTINE GP/dermatology referral (1–2 weeks), i.e. T1. Despite the alarming, high-distress framing and the source 'Urgency Justification', the skin condition itself does not warrant urgent same-day in-person review or emergency escalation; over-triaging it is the primary scored defect. The acute PANIC ATTACK is managed in-session with de-escalation and settles with reassurance and controlled breathing, and does not itself require an ambulance in the absence of cardiorespiratory red flags. The essential conditional escalation is for the PSYCHOLOGICAL risk (self-harm / inability to cope), which is screened negative here but must be explicitly safety-netted. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass is therefore T1 — a correct recognise + reassure + routine-refer + mental-health-safety-net answer passes.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Thoughts of self-harm or suicide, feeling unable to keep oneself safe, or overwhelming, uncontrollable distress that does not settle with reassurance and breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately, the moment such thoughts or a sense of being unable to cope arise
escalation_edges.rationale: An acute health-anxiety crisis carries a small but real self-harm risk; any positive self-harm ideation or inability to stay safe converts this into a mental-health emergency requiring immediate crisis support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If at any point you have thoughts of harming yourself, feel you can't keep yourself safe, or feel completely overwhelmed and unable to cope, please call a mental-health crisis line or 000 straight away, or go to your nearest emergency department — you deserve immediate support.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe chest pain, fainting or collapse, or genuine breathlessness that is not just the fast breathing of the panic
escalation_edges.rationale: Panic symptoms overlap with cardiorespiratory emergencies; true chest pain, syncope or breathlessness needs urgent in-person assessment to exclude a cardiac or respiratory cause, escalating to 000 if severe.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop real chest pain, feel like you're going to pass out or actually collapse, or you're genuinely struggling to breathe rather than just breathing fast — treat that as an emergency and call 000 or get to an emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Numbness or loss of feeling developing within the patches, or a raised, red, active spreading border
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Prompt GP review within 24–48 hours if this appears
escalation_edges.rationale: Loss of sensation or an active border would point away from benign depigmentation toward an alternative diagnosis such as leprosy that needs in-person examination and specific testing.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If any of the white areas start to feel numb, or you notice a raised red active edge to them, see your GP within a day or two so they can examine and test the skin properly.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New scaling, itch, pain, blistering, ulceration or bleeding in the patches, or a mole/pigmented spot that is changing, growing or bleeding; or severe sunburn/blistering of a patch
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: GP review within 24–48 hours (routine dermatology review otherwise)
escalation_edges.rationale: Atypical or changing features suggest an inflammatory, infective or neoplastic process rather than stable vitiligo and warrant an in-person skin examination.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the patches start to flake, itch, hurt, blister, ulcerate or bleed, or you notice a mole or spot that is changing, growing or bleeding, or a patch gets badly sunburnt, book a GP review within a day or two to have the skin looked at.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Acute severe panic attack with a catastrophic fear of a 'flesh-eating' disfiguring disease, alone at home with no one else present
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Because the patient is alone and acutely distressed, lead with calm, explicit reassurance and de-escalation (slow controlled breathing) BEFORE detailed explanation, and pair it with an explicit self-harm screen and a concrete mental-health crisis safety-net so reassurance is never mistaken for dismissal. This reassurance is itself therapeutic and reduces the risk of both the patient and the clinician over-escalating the benign skin condition.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: An acute reactive panic / health-anxiety crisis overlaying an otherwise benign, chronic skin condition
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise treating the acute anxiety (reassurance + controlled breathing) as the same-encounter task while managing the skin condition routinely; resist letting the alarming distress and the inability to physically examine drive over-escalation of the benign dermatosis to urgent in-person or emergency care.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: These white patches are a harmless loss of skin pigment — not an infection, cancer or 'flesh-eating' disease — and the skin itself is healthy. Settle the panic now with slow, controlled breathing, protect the patches with daily SPF 50+ sunscreen, and see your GP within 1–2 weeks for some baseline blood tests (including a thyroid check), psychological support, and a referral to a dermatologist for treatments that can help. BUT if you ever have thoughts of harming yourself, feel unable to cope, or develop real chest pain, faintness or genuine difficulty breathing — call a mental-health crisis line or 000 immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Routine GP appointment (or telehealth re-contact) for the skin and psychological support; a mental-health crisis line or 000 immediately for self-harm thoughts, inability to cope, or cardiorespiratory red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: For the skin condition and general support out of hours, use an after-hours GP service or a nurse advice line; for thoughts of self-harm or overwhelming distress, contact a mental-health crisis line or 000 at any hour without waiting for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document classic non-segmental vitiligo (periorificial + acral depigmentation, leukotrichia, normal sensation, no scaling) presenting with an acute reactive panic attack. Recommend a baseline autoimmune/thyroid screen, psychological support / Mental Health Treatment Plan, dermatology referral for repigmentation options, and strict photoprotection of the depigmented skin.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot formally test sensation, palpate nerves or perform a Wood's-lamp exam to definitively exclude the leprosy and fungal mimics.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Rely on the classic morphology plus patient-reported normal sensation to make a confident benign working diagnosis, manage with reassurance + sun protection, and arrange routine in-person GP/dermatology review to complete the examination — while safety-netting the specific features (sensory loss, active border) that would change the diagnosis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the inability to examine as a reason to escalate the benign skin condition to urgent same-day in-person review, an ED or an ambulance, or to treat a feared infection empirically with antifungals/antibiotics.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the encounter as pure dermatological self-care with NO self-harm screen and NO mental-health crisis safety-net for the acute severe distress.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An acutely distressed, panicking patient alone at home is left without a crisis pathway; escalating self-harm risk goes unrecognised and unsupported.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Correct low-acuity dermatological disposition but omitting either the explicit self-harm safety-net or the in-session panic de-escalation, leaving the psychological crisis under-supported.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Escalating the benign vitiligo to urgent same-day in-person review, ED or an ambulance, or empirically treating a feared infection, despite no mental-health or cardiorespiratory red flag — the primary over-triage defect; causes unnecessary emergency resource use and reinforces the patient's catastrophic health anxiety.
